pet.name: Terror
pet.special_needs: -  Mild weight-management needs. Gets tired in hot weather, so exercise should stay short and low intensity. Watch breathing during walks and avoid overheating.
pet.characteristics: Heat and Breathing: Pugs are brachycephalic and overheat easily; keep exercise short, low-intensity, and avoid hot weather or midday activity. Monitor for labored breathing, wheezing, or excessive panting during and after walks, and stop immediately if breathing becomes strained. || Weight and Mobility: Pugs are prone to weight gain, which compounds joint stress and breathing difficulty; maintain consistent portion control and low-impact activity to protect their compact frame and reduce strain on the spine and knees. || Grooming and Skin: Pugs have facial wrinkles and skin folds that trap moisture and debris; clean and dry these areas regularly to prevent infection, and brush their short coat weekly to manage shedding.
pet.species_profile_summary: Pugs typically live 12 to 15 years and require careful management of heat exposure, breathing, weight, and skin-fold hygiene due to their brachycephalic build.

age_context.summary: Pugs typically live 12 to 15 years and require careful management of heat exposure, breathing, weight, and skin-fold hygiene due to their brachycephalic build.

goal: Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance.
extra_context: -  Build a practical routine for a 6-year-old pug with short daily walks, indoor enrichment, feeding consistency, weight management, and reminders for hot-weather safety.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning low-intensity walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Short walk in cool morning hours to avoid heat stress; monitor breathing and stop if panting becomes labored.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Pugs overheat easily and need brief, low-intensity activity; morning timing avoids midday heat.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening low-intensity walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Short walk in cool evening hours; watch for labored breathing and excessive panting.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Second brief walk supports weight management and enrichment without overheating risk.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning meal with portion control
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed measured portion to support weight management; follow veterinary feeding plan.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent portion control is essential for weight management in weight-prone pugs.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening meal with portion control
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed measured portion to support weight management; follow veterinary feeding plan.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Second measured meal maintains consistent feeding schedule and weight control.

raw_recommendations.name: Daily observation and monitoring
raw_recommendations.notes: Record appetite, water intake, energy level, and bathroom habits; note any changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent observation creates a record of health trends and catches early changes in a special-needs pet.

raw_recommendations.name: Indoor enrichment and play
raw_recommendations.notes: Short interactive play sessions indoors to avoid heat; stop before exhaustion.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Brief enrichment supports mental stimulation and weight management without overheating risk.

raw_recommendations.name: Facial wrinkle and skin-fold cleaning
raw_recommendations.notes: Clean and dry facial wrinkles and skin folds to prevent moisture and debris buildup.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Pugs have facial wrinkles that trap moisture and debris; daily cleaning prevents infection.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly body-condition and weight check
raw_recommendations.notes: Assess body condition and note any weight changes; compare to previous weeks.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular weight monitoring helps track mild weight-management needs and adjust care as needed.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly coat brushing
raw_recommendations.notes: Brush short coat to manage shedding and check skin condition.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weekly brushing manages pug shedding and allows early detection of skin issues.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly heat-safety and breathing review
raw_recommendations.notes: Review heat-exposure patterns, breathing observations, and activity adjustments for the month.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Monthly trend review helps catch early signs of heat stress or breathing changes in a brachycephalic breed.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly weight-management trend check
raw_recommendations.notes: Review weekly weight and body-condition notes; assess if portion control or activity needs adjustment.
raw_recommendations.rationale: End-of-month review of weight trends informs adjustments to feeding or exercise before issues compound.

raw_recommendations.name: Labored breathing or heat distress response
raw_recommendations.notes: If Terror shows labored breathing, excessive panting, or signs of

raw_recommendations.name: Monitor breathing during walks and stop if labored
raw_recommendations.notes: Watch for wheezing, excessive panting, or strained breathing; stop immediately if any sign appears.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Pugs are brachycephalic and overheat easily; breathing distress during or after walks is a trigger to cease activity and cool Terror down.

raw_recommendations.name: Adjust exercise intensity or skip walks on hot days
raw_recommendations.notes: On hot or midday weather, reduce walk duration further or move to early morning or evening; watch for overheating signs.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Heat exposure triggers breathing and overheating risk in pugs; proactive schedule shifts protect Terror during warm seasons.

raw_recommendations.name: Increase wrinkle cleaning if moisture or odor appears
raw_recommendations.notes: If facial folds show excess moisture, redness, or odor between daily cleanings, clean and dry immediately to prevent infection.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Pug skin folds trap moisture and debris; visible signs of moisture buildup or odor signal infection risk and require prompt intervention.

raw_recommendations.name: Contact veterinarian if appetite or energy drops suddenly
raw_recommendations.notes: If Terror refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline in energy or bathroom habits, contact a veterinarian promptly.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Sudden changes in appetite, energy, or bathroom habits in older pets warrant veterinary evaluation to rule out underlying issues.

raw_recommendations.name: Watch for gait changes or mobility difficulty
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe for limping, stiffness, confusion, or difficulty with stairs or transitions; report major changes to veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Older pugs are prone to joint stress from weight and age; early detection of mobility changes helps prevent injury or pain.

raw_recommendations.name: Monitor water intake and adjust if excessive thirst appears
raw_recommendations.notes: Track daily water intake; if Terror drinks significantly more than usual, note and report to veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Sudden changes in water intake can signal health issues; observation creates a record for veterinary review.

raw_recommendations.name: Provide extra rest time if panting or fatigue persists after activity
raw_recommendations.notes: If Terror continues panting or appears fatigued well after a walk ends, allow extended cool-down and rest indoors.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Brachycephalic breeds need longer recovery time; prolonged panting signals overexertion and requires immediate rest and cooling.

raw_recommendations.name: Reduce meal portions if weight gain is detected at weekly check
raw_recommendations.notes: If weekly body-condition check shows weight increase, reduce portion size slightly; do not change without monitoring trend.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight gain in pugs compounds joint stress and breathing difficulty; portion adjustment triggered by weekly monitoring protects Terror's health.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening low-intensity walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Second daily walk during cooler evening hours to support weight management and low-impact activity.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Pugs need consistent low-intensity exercise split across the day to manage weight without overheating; evening walk complements morning routine.

raw_recommendations.name: Midday breathing and temperature check
raw_recommendations.notes: Quick observation of Terror's breathing, panting rate, and comfort level during warmest part of day.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Brachycephalic pugs are heat-sensitive; midday check ensures no signs of overheating or respiratory distress before afternoon enrichment.

raw_recommendations.name: Afternoon snack and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer water and a small measured snack to support consistent calorie distribution and hydration throughout the day.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Weight management requires portion control across multiple small meals; afternoon snack prevents hunger while maintaining calorie targets.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle mobility and stretching session
raw_recommendations.notes: Slow, low-impact movement such as gentle play or short indoor exploration to support joint health without overexertion.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Pugs are prone to joint stress from weight and compact frame; daily gentle mobility maintains flexibility and reduces spine and knee strain.

raw_recommendations.name: Calm settling and rest routine
raw_recommendations.notes: Quiet time with soft music, puzzle toy, or calm companionship to encourage relaxation before evening.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Enrichment should include calm moments to prevent overstimulation; supports weight management by reducing stress-related eating.

raw_recommendations.name: Bedtime breathing and comfort check
raw_recommendations.notes: Final observation of Terror's breathing, posture, and comfort level before sleep to catch any respiratory or distress signs early.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Brachycephalic dogs may show breathing changes during rest; consistent evening check creates early-warning record for veterinary discussion.

raw_recommendations.name: Training and mental enrichment session
raw_recommendations.notes: Short, reward-based training or nose-work game to engage mind without physical overexertion.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Mental stimulation supports weight management by reducing boredom-driven eating and provides low-impact enrichment suitable for heat-sensitive pugs.

raw_recommendations.name: Appetite and energy log entry
raw_recommendations.notes: Brief daily note on Terror's meal completion, water intake, and overall energy to track trends for weight and health.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Observation records create baseline for detecting sudden appetite or energy drops that warrant veterinary contact.
